title: Oreocnide fruticosa
intro: Oreocnide fruticosa, also known as the Chinese ivy or Korean ivy, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Urticaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreocnide fruticosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal preparations, Oreocnide fruticosa is often made into a decoction or a tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Oreocnide fruticosa is classified within the family Urticaceae. This family includes stinging nettle and other plants known for their stinging hairs. Oreocnide fruticosa is a type of vine native to Asia. It is also known as the Asian stinging vine due to its stinging hairs. The plant is a member of the genus Oreocnide, which consists of several species of stinging vines.
